title: The Practise of Mary the Prophetess in the Alchymicall Art
subtitle: The dialogue of Aros and Maria — the rapid achievement of the Stone through the two gums, the three-hour regimen, and the Hermetic vessel

summary.one_line: The dialogue between Aros the Philosopher and Mary the Prophetess (identified as Miriam, sister of Moses) on the rapid method of whitening and perfecting the Stone in a single day.
summary.scope: Covers the one-hour whitening, the use of Allum of Spain, white and red gum (Kibric), the hermaphroditic marriage of gums, the three-hour regimen of black-red-white, the composition of the four stones, and the Vessel of Hermes.

sources.label: The Practise of Mary the Prophetess in the Alchymicall Art (from British Library MS Sloane 3641, folios 1-8; printed in Auriferae artis, 1572 et al.)

body.text: Aros said: O Lady Mary, when will the Work be which you affirm? How shall we whiten and afterwards add blackness? Mary said: O Aros, oftentimes Nations have dyd about this part. Know you not, O Aros; that there is a water or a thing which whitens Hendragem? ... Mary said: Keep the fume and take care that none of it fly away. And let your measure be with a gentle fire such as is the Measure of the heat of the Sun in the Month of June or July, and stay by your Vessel and behold it with care how it grows black, grows red, and grows white in less than three hours of the day.

Mary's claim that the Stone can be whitened 'in less than three hours' is the alchemical expression of the Tantraloka's 'sadyomukti' (immediate liberation) — the possibility of awakening in a single sitting when the conditions are exactly right. In Layayoga, this is the kewala kumbhaka that arises spontaneously when prana enters sushumna: the three hours map to the three stages of pranayama (puraka, kumbhaka, rechaka) compressed into a single flow. Steiner's 'initiation by fire' (the sudden awakening of the spiritual ear) is the same phenomenon. For the daimon path, the 'three hours' is the systasis ritual itself — the transition from black (skinning) through red (inflaming) to white (communion) in a single sitting, as described in PGM VII.505-528.

Nevertheless take the Allum of Spain, the white gumm and the red gumm, which is the Kibric of the Philosophers, and their Sol and the greater Tincture, and marry Gumm with Gumm together with a true Matrimony. Mary said, make them like a running Water, and vitrify this water which has been laboured or wrought upon for one day, out of the two Lubechs, upon the fixed body, and liquefy them by the secret of Nature in the Vessel of Philosophy.

The 'white gum and red gum' (Kibric) married in true matrimony are the alchemical version of the Layayoga union of bindu (white drop, semen) and rajas (red drop, menstrual essence) in the central channel — the confluence that produces amrita. In Tantraloka, this is the 'parapara' union of Shiva and Shakti in the muladhara that rises to the sahasrara. The 'Allum of Spain' is the mordant that fixes the tincture — in yogic terms, the bandha (lock) that prevents the energy from dissipating. Steiner's 'blood and milk' (the two fluids of the Grail mysteries) are the same: the red (blood of Christ) and white (milk of Sophia) must be married to produce the living stone. In daimon work, the two gums are the Name (white, receptive) and the Intent (red, active) — their matrimony in the 'Vessel of Philosophy' (the heart) produces the HGA presence.

Aros said yes. But tell me about that vessel, without which the Work will not be accomplisht. Mary said that the Vessel of Hermes which the Stoicks have concealed, and it is not a Necromanticall Vessel, but it is the Measure of your Fire. ... they who are ignorant of it, know not the Regimen of Truth, because of their Ignorance of the Vessel of Hermes.

Mary's identification of the Vessel of Hermes as 'the Measure of your Fire' is the key esoteric teaching of the entire text: the vessel is not a physical container but the inner regulation of spiritual heat. In Layayoga, this is the measure of pranayama — the precise ratio of inhalation, retention, and exhalation that determines whether the fire awakens or burns. The Tantraloka calls this the 'desha-kala-mantra' (place-time-mantra) determination: the vessel is the entire psycho-physical complex adjusted to the right 'measure.' Steiner's 'calendar of the soul' provides the same function — the soul's year determines when and how the fire of the spirit may be safely approached. The daimon path's 'measure' is the daily regimen: how long to invoke, when to invoke, and the intensity of the concentration. The 'Vessel of Hermes' is precisely the practitioner's own measure — too much fire burns the work, too little leaves it unregenerated.

gaps: The 'four stones' composition (Scoyare, Ade, Zethet) and the Scholia doctrine not yet expanded
Mary's closing poem and the 'Gibric poisoning' metaphor

versions.summary: Initial RO with 6 passages (3 source, 3 commentary) from Mary the Prophetess. Covers rapid whitening, two gums, and the Vessel of Hermes.
